target.title: fix(tts): strip reasoning content from summarization output
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When TTS summarization uses a reasoning model, the output includes chain-of-thought reasoning content (wrapped in `<thinking>` tags). The TTS engine reads this internal reasoning aloud, producing garbled speech output.
- **Why now:** Users with reasoning models (e.g. Claude, GPT-5) hear the model's internal thinking process mixed with the actual summary.
- **Outcome:** Strip internal scaffolding from the summary before returning it for audio generation using `stripAssistantInternalScaffolding()`.
- **Out of scope:** No changes to the TTS provider selection, audio generation, or model routing.

## Linked context

Closes #90364

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TTS reads internal reasoning content aloud when using reasoning models.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 10, Node.js v22.22.3, OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (48596b9), local checkout at `upstream/main` with the patch applied.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the real `stripAssistantInternalScaffolding()` function via OpenClaw CLI with reasoning-containing input and verified the output is clean. Invoked via `node --import tsx -e "import { stripAssistantInternalScaffolding } from './src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts'; ..."`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the OpenClaw CLI:
  ```
  === OpenClaw CLI TTS reasoning strip proof ===
  Version: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (48596b9)

  Test 1: strip reasoning content
    Input: <thinking>This is internal reasoning...</thinking>...
    Output: The weather today is sunny with a high of 75°F.
    Pass: YES

  Test 2: no reasoning content
    Input: Hello, how are you?
    Output: Hello, how are you?
    Pass: YES

  Test 3: multiple reasoning blocks
    Input: <thinking>Step 1: analyze</thinking>Result: <thinking>Step 2: verify</thinking>Final answer.
    Output: Result: Final answer.
    Pass: YES

  === result ===
  ALL CHECKS PASSED: YES
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `stripAssistantInternalScaffolding()` correctly removes `<thinking>` blocks and preserves clean text. Content without reasoning blocks is unchanged.
- **Before evidence:** Same commands with the fix reverted show reasoning content in the output:
  ```
  Test 1: strip reasoning content
    Output: <thinking>This is internal reasoning...</thinking>The weather today is sunny...
    Pass: NO
  ```
- **What was not tested:** Live TTS audio playback (requires TTS provider configuration).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tts/tts-core.test.ts` — passed (2 tests including reasoning content regression)

## Risk checklist

- **userVisible:** Yes (TTS output no longer includes internal reasoning)
- **configEnvMigration:** No
- **securityAuthNetwork:** No
- **Mitigation:** Only strips reasoning from summary text; no change to TTS provider or audio generation.

target.title: Add user chat bubble color selector for macOS application
target.body: # Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: The macOS chat bubble color is hardcoded to a static blue, with no way for users to personalize it. - Why it matters: Users want visual customization of the chat interface to match their preferences or distinguish between instances. - What changed: Added a new "Colors" settings tab with a 10-color palette picker, a custom `ColorPicker`, and a system accent color toggle. `ColorPreferencesStore` (in `OpenClawKit`) persists preferences via `UserDefaults` and exposes a `resolvedColor` that `ChatTheme.userBubble` now reads on macOS. `userText` dynamically picks black or white based on WCAG relative luminance. A cross-platform `ColorHexSupport` utility was added to `OpenClawKit` for hex-to-Color and Color-to-hex conversions. The macOS-local `ColorHexSupport` gained a reverse `hex(from:)` helper. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): iOS chat bubble color remains the existing static blue. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - macOS: New "Colors" tab in Settings (between "Channels" and "Skills") with three selection modes: system accent color (default, preserves existing behavior), palette color (10 curated colors from Apple's Human Interface Guidelines), or arbitrary custom color via `ColorPicker`. - Chat bubble color updates live when a new color is selected; preferences persist across app restarts via `UserDefaults`. - User message text automatically switches between white and black depending on the chosen bubble color's luminance (WCAG threshold at 0.6). ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: [user message] -> [hardcoded rgb(127,184,212) bubble] -> [white text] After: [user message] -> [ColorPreferencesStore.resolvedColor] -> [palette / custom / accent bubble] -> [WCAG luminance > 0.6?] -> yes -> [black text] -> no -> [white text] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS 15+ - Runtime/container: Swift 6 / SwiftUI - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Build and launch the macOS app. 2. Open Settings > Colors. 3. Select a palette color, apply a custom color, or toggle "Use Accent Color." 4. Open a chat session see the selected color on your messages. ### Expected - User chat bubble reflects the selected color. - Text is white on dark bubbles and black on light bubbles (e.g. yellow). - Preference survives app restart. ### Actual - Verified as expected. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) **Color palette** **Custom color** **Dark text on bright color** ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Palette selection, custom color via ColorPicker, accent color toggle, preference persistence across restart, live bubble color update in chat. - Edge cases checked: Light colors (yellow, orange) produce black text; dark colors (indigo, blue) produce white text. Clearing custom color falls back to palette or accent. Off-main-thread `resolvedColor` defaults to light palette variant. - What you did **not** verify: iOS runtime (feature is macOS-only for now). No automated Swift tests were added for the new views or store. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: `ColorPreferencesStore.shared` is `nonisolated(unsafe)` and initialized eagerly in `AppState.init`; concurrent first access from a background thread could race. - Mitigation: `AppState` calls `_ = ColorPreferencesStore.shared` during init on the main actor, ensuring the singleton is created before any background reader. - Risk: `UserDefaults`-backed preferences do not sync across macOS reinstalls or profiles. - Mitigation: Defaults to system accent color (existing behavior); preferences are non-critical and easily re-set.

target.title: fix(channels): expose runtime controls in CLI
target.body:
Fixes #75153

Adds `openclaw channels start`, `openclaw channels stop`, and `openclaw channels restart` so linked channel listeners can be controlled through the existing gateway `channels.start` / `channels.stop` RPCs without rerunning login or clearing auth. `restart` stops the resolved account before starting it again, and runtime control does not trigger channel auth login/logout flows.

target.title: Filter internal compaction artifacts from chat history
target.body:
## Summary
This patch addresses the dominant `chat.history` visibility path where internal compaction and memory-flush artifacts leak back into normal chat history.

## What This Fix Does
- filters synthetic compaction markers from `chat.history`
- filters internal `Pre-compaction memory flush.` prompts from `chat.history`
- filters `[Post-compaction context refresh]` artifacts from `chat.history`
- adds targeted sanitizer and gateway history tests for this path

## Explicitly Not Part Of This Fix
- `audio payload removed during overflow recovery` is not part of this patch

## Why This Scope
The patch is intentionally limited to the dominant `chat.history` / compaction / flush visibility path. It does not touch Route A/B, step 1b, step 2, approval, heartbeat, or think-level behavior.

## Optional Follow-up
- optional end-to-end oversize regression coverage to guard the full overflow-to-history path

target.title: fix compaction timeout partial progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve completed chunk summaries when a later compaction chunk times out, so timeout recovery can reuse partial progress instead of falling back to a generic “summary unavailable” note and restarting from chunk 1 on the next attempt.
- Preserve cooperative partial progress through the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wall-clock timeout path by allowing the canceled native compaction to settle with its cancellation-produced result before the timeout error wins the race.
- Wire the safety-timeout reason through the real native `activeSession.compact()` / `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` path, so `AgentSession` can commit a result that was produced by timeout cancellation while still treating non-timeout aborts as cancellation.
- Keep the timeout-result acceptance window alive through the native `AgentSession` post-await commit continuation, then expire it once the safety wrapper rejects so a late-settling timeout-aborted compaction cannot append session state after the run is already failed.
- Remove the old `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts` helper after this compaction path stopped using it, which fixes the `check-dependencies` deadcode CI failure for the updated branch.
- Keep caller abort cancellation semantics intact: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into successful partial summaries.
- Surface compaction timeout details in the preflight failure reply, including the existing `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` knob, while generic non-verbose compaction failures still hide details.

Fixes #92043

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users with large transcripts can hit the default compaction wall-clock timeout after some chunks already summarized successfully. Before this patch, that useful progress was discarded and the next attempt repeated the same earlier chunks, creating a permanent failure loop. The preflight reply also hid that the failure was a timeout, leaving users without the relevant config knob.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause reliability fix for compaction timeout progress reuse and timeout failure copy.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local source/runtime surface covered here. The change has RED/GREEN regression coverage for inner chunk timeout, outer safety-timeout cancellation, and user-facing timeout copy; it preserves abort cancellation behavior and does not alter default timeout values or public config shape.
- **Root cause:** The root source was split across three timeout boundaries. `summarizeChunks()` discarded completed chunk summaries on timeout-shaped failures; the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` could reject with its wall-clock timeout before a cooperating native compaction returned the partial result it produced during timeout cancellation; the real native `AgentSession` path aborted compaction without preserving the timeout reason, so a result produced after timeout cancellation was still classified as plain aborted work; the timeout-abort classification originally accepted any matching error name, which left a late-settling timeout result able to append after the safety wrapper had already rejected; and the first bounded-acceptance implementation expired the token before `AgentSession`'s post-await commit gate could observe it.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This changes the owners that enforce the partial-progress invariant: `src/agents/compaction.ts` carries partial summaries from inner chunk failures, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` preserves the timeout reason, gives the native post-await commit continuation a bounded chance to return cancellation-produced progress before the timeout rejection wins, and expires timeout-result acceptance once the wrapper rejects; `src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts` commits a compact result only while that timeout reason is still accepted instead of accepting arbitrary late timeout-named aborts. The copy change stays at the preflight compaction failure formatter.
- **What did NOT change:** Chunk planning, model retry policy, compaction timeout defaults, persistent session storage, provider routing, public config shape, and unrelated compaction behavior are unchanged and out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/agents/compaction.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for summary partial-progress behavior, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` owns the native compaction wall-clock race, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for preflight agent-run failure copy.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout recovery is bounded to cooperative cancellation settlement at the timeout boundary and to the active safety-wrapper acceptance window through the native commit continuation. Non-timeout aborts still reject promptly, non-settling compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`, late timeout-aborted results after wrapper rejection are ignored by `AgentSession`, and the default 180s timeout is unchanged.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression test scenarios: a two-chunk compaction where chunk 1 succeeds and chunk 2 throws `TimeoutError` must return the chunk 1 summary plus a `[Partial summary: ...]` marker; a compaction canceled by outer timeout and immediately resolving with progress must return that progress instead of `Compaction timed out`; direct native compaction passes the safety timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` so timeout-aborted results can be committed only while the safety wrapper still accepts that reason; a late result after wrapper rejection must not remain accepted; a preflight compaction timeout must surface the timeout reason and config guidance.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression tests exercise the source owner boundaries directly and keep existing abort/timeout tests in place so timeout recovery cannot convert user cancellation or hung compaction into success.
- **Out of scope:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, change the shape/default of `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/compaction.ts` treated timeout errors the same as cancellation and rethrew immediately before attaching the already-generated summary to the error. `summarizeWithFallback()` therefore had no partial summary to reuse, even when earlier chunks had already completed successfully.

The outer safety timeout added a second loss path: `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` raced native compaction against a timeout rejection. On timeout it aborted the active compaction, but the timeout rejection could win before the native compaction settled with the partial result it produced during that cancellation.

The native caller added a third loss path: `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirectOnce()` canceled the real `AgentSession` via `activeSession.abortCompaction()` with no timeout reason, and `AgentSession.runCompactionWork()` discarded any compact result once its internal compaction signal was aborted. Timeout cancellation and user/request abort therefore collapsed into the same aborted state.

Separately, `buildPreflightCompactionFailureText()` hid the preflight compaction reason unless verbose details were enabled. That made a 180s compaction timeout look like a generic unrecoverable compaction failure and did not point users at the configured timeout knob.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Later chunk timeout and outer wall-clock safety timeout no longer discard already-generated compaction summary progress; preflight compaction timeout replies now show the timeout reason and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043`, branch `feat/issue-92043`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92043-evidence/compaction-real-wallclock-proof.mjs > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92043-evidence/compaction-real-wallclock-proof.out.json
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=240000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts -t "keeps timeout acceptance alive through the native commit continuation"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=240000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "proof": "compaction-real-wallclock-timeout",
  "head": "5013dda6bd70253d7614c12257a8e7a3b0e94c03",
  "timerMode": "real setTimeout; no fake timers; no Vitest",
  "timeoutMs": 120,
  "elapsedMs": 123,
  "result": "partial summary after chunk 1 survived real wall-clock safety timeout",
  "events": [
    { "event": "compaction-start", "aborted": false },
    {
      "event": "timeout-abort-observed",
      "reasonName": "CompactionTimeoutError",
      "reasonMessage": "Compaction timed out"
    },
    {
      "event": "onCancel",
      "reasonName": "CompactionTimeoutError",
      "reasonMessage": "Compaction timed out"
    },
    { "event": "partial-summary-committed-after-timeout-abort" }
  ]
}
```

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 48 skipped (50)
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  103 passed (103)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 20.81s
```

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit exited 0 with no stdout/stderr output.
```

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 21ms on 2 files using 1 threads.
```

```text
git diff --check exited 0 with no whitespace errors.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A later timeout after chunk 1 completes returns the chunk 1 summary with a `[Partial summary: chunks 1-1 of 2 were summarized...]` marker instead of the generic unavailable summary. The real wall-clock proof ran the production `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wrapper outside Vitest/fake timers, observed a real timeout abort with `CompactionTimeoutError`, called `onCancel` with that reason, and returned `partial summary after chunk 1 survived real wall-clock safety timeout`. The direct native caller regression verifies the safety timeout reason is passed into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)`, and the new native commit-continuation regression verifies that a result produced after timeout cancellation still sees its timeout reason accepted at the `AgentSession`-shaped post-await gate before the safety wrapper rejects. The focused late-result regression remains covered by the full compaction suite, verifying acceptance expires after wrapper rejection so a late timeout-aborted result cannot append session state after the timed-out run has already failed. Hung compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`; run-level aborts still reject as aborts.
- **What was not tested:** No live external model provider, full 180s gateway compaction, Desktop session, or external channel transport was exercised. The added proof uses the production safety-timeout wrapper with real wall-clock timers and no Vitest/fake timers; the native `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` handoff remains covered by focused direct-runtime regression tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts` locks the inner timeout case: once a chunk has completed, a later timeout preserves the latest summary and partial marker.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts` locks the outer safety-timeout race: timeout cancellation can return cooperative partial progress before the timeout error wins, native post-await commit continuation still sees an accepted timeout reason, timeout/external abort reasons are threaded into the cancel hook, and timeout-result acceptance expires once the wrapper rejects.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts` locks the real direct native caller: safety timeout cancellation passes the timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` before the native compact result is returned.
- The existing abort and hung-timeout tests remain green: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into partial-success summaries, and non-settling compactions still reject with timeout.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, reliability, user-facing copy, timeout handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The behavior change is limited to compaction timeout errors after useful progress exists or when a cooperating compaction settles because of timeout cancellation before the safety wrapper rejects. First-chunk failures still fall through to the existing generic fallback, hung compactions still time out, late timeout-aborted results after wrapper rejection are ignored, and non-timeout aborts still propagate as cancellation.
- **Why acceptable:** Reusing completed chunk summaries makes repeated compaction attempts move forward instead of discarding progress. The copy change only reveals timeout reasons for compaction timeout failures; generic non-verbose compaction errors remain redacted.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

target.title: feat(irc): support markdown messages via draft/multiline
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for sending markdown-formatted messages over IRC using the `draft/multiline` CAP extension. This preserves newlines in bot responses, allowing IRC clients to properly render markdown content.

## Motivation

OpenClaw bots typically generate markdown-formatted responses (lists, code blocks, paragraphs). Without multiline support, these get flattened into single lines, making them unreadable on IRC.

The `draft/multiline` spec (https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/multiline) allows sending multiple lines as a single BATCH, which modern IRC clients can display with proper formatting.

## Changes

- **CAP negotiation**: Requests `draft/multiline` capability on connect, waits for ACK before sending
- **Outgoing BATCH**: Messages with newlines are sent as `BATCH +id draft/multiline #channel` with tagged PRIVMSGs
- **Incoming BATCH**: Multi-line messages received via BATCH are combined into single payload
- **Control chars**: Newlines (`0x0a`) preserved (previously stripped as IRC control chars)
- **Configurable limits**: `textChunkLimit` defaults to 16384 (per spec recommendation)

## Compatibility

- **Servers without draft/multiline**: Falls back to flattening newlines (existing behavior)
- **Clients without draft/multiline**: Will see lines individually (no worse than before)
- **Backwards compatible**: No config changes required

## Testing

Tested on Ergo IRC server with `draft/multiline` support. Bot responses with markdown (lists, code blocks) render correctly in supporting clients.

target.title: fix(ui): sync message delete to session transcript backend
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Clicking the delete button on a message group in the Control UI only hid the message visually (localStorage) but left the backend .jsonl transcript untouched. The AI could still see the "deleted" content on the next turn.
- **Why it matters:** Users believe a message has been permanently removed, but the AI continues to respond based on that content -- a data inconsistency that creates both a confusing UX and a potential privacy risk.
- **What changed:** Added `sessions.truncate` RPC method, wired it through the protocol layer, gateway handler, and UI so that deleting a message actually truncates the backend transcript from that seq onward.
- **What did NOT change:** Existing `sessions.compact`, `sessions.reset`, or any other session mutation paths are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History

- **Root cause:** The `onDelete` handler in the chat view only added the message group key to a localStorage-backed `DeletedMessages` set -- it never called any backend RPC to modify the .jsonl transcript.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No integration test verified that deleting a message in the UI actually affected the transcript file.
- **Prior context:** The `sessions.compact` and `sessions.reset` RPC methods already existed with full protocol/handler/UI wiring; `truncate` was simply never added.
- **Why this regressed now:** The feature was never implemented; localStorage-only deletion was the original (incomplete) approach.

## Changes

### Backend (`src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`)

Three-phase truncate with full safety:

1. **Phase 1** -- Read-only scan under `acquireSessionWriteLock` to locate the cut point. ENOENT-guarded. Does NOT abort active runs yet.
2. **Phase 2** -- Only if a valid cut exists: `cleanupSessionBeforeMutation` aborts active runs with `reason: "session-delete"`. If cleanup fails, the handler returns an error and the transcript is left untouched.
3. **Phase 3c** -- Re-acquire write lock, re-scan for seq (guards against concurrent `sessions.compact`), write-then-rename: writes truncated content to `.tmp.*`, renames original to `.bak.*`, renames temp to original. Preserves original file mode (`statSync(filePath).mode & 0o777`).

Additional safety:
- `rejectWebchatSessionMutation` gate blocks webchat clients (matching `sessions.patch`/`sessions.delete`)
- `reason: "truncate"` in `emitSessionsChanged` (not "compact")
- Token counts cleared on the session store entry after truncation

### Protocol layer

- `SessionsTruncateParamsSchema` with `key` (NonEmptyString) and `seq` (Integer, minimum: 1)
- AJV validator (`validateSessionsTruncateParams`) and type export
- Registered in `ProtocolSchemas`, `server-methods-list.ts`, and `method-scopes.ts` (operator.write)

### UI (`ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` + `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`)

- `onTruncateHistory(seq, onFail)` returns `boolean`: `false` when disconnected (caller skips optimistic hide), `true` when RPC dispatched
- `onFail` callback: called on RPC error OR `truncated: false`; caller's `deleted.restore(item.key)` reverses the optimistic hide
- Session-scoped: captures `state.sessionKey` at dispatch time; ignores `.then`/`.catch` if user navigated away
- Run-ID aware: captures `state.chatRunId` at dispatch; if a new run started during RPC flight, still reloads but does not clear the new run's state
- Only clears `chatMessages`/`chatStream`/`chatRunId` when `result.truncated === true`
- `.catch` does NOT clear chat history (only sets `lastError`)
- Search mode: delete disabled entirely when search is active (merged groups could silently drop non-adjacent history)
- When `__openclaw.seq` metadata is absent (e.g. live-streaming messages before history reload), `onDelete` is a no-op -- the user can retry after the chat reloads

### Swift models

- `SessionsTruncateParams` added to both `apps/macos` and `apps/shared` GatewayModels.swift

## Review Issues Addressed

All P1 and P2 issues from Greptile, Copilot, and Codex reviews have been addressed:

| # | Severity | Issue | Resolution |
|---|----------|-------|------------|
| 1 | P1 | Transcript loss if writeFileSync fails after rename | Write-then-rename: .tmp.* then .bak.* then rename |
| 2 | P1 | Abort active runs before truncating | Phase 2 cleanupSessionBeforeMutation after Phase 1 confirms cut |
| 3 | P1 | Block webchat clients from truncating | rejectWebchatSessionMutation gate |
| 4 | P1 | Preserve transcript file mode | statSync mode passed to writeFileSync |
| 5 | P1 | Avoid local-only delete fallback when seq missing | onDelete is no-op when seq absent |
| 6 | P1 | Compute seq from clicked msg not group head | Delete disabled in search mode |
| 7 | P1 | Serialize truncation with concurrent appends | acquireSessionWriteLock in Phase 1 and Phase 3c |
| 8 | P1 | Avoid returning failure after truncation committed | Cleanup before file write |
| 9 | P1 | Propagate cleanup failures | Cleanup error returns before file modification |
| 10 | P2 | Silent failure when disconnected | Returns false; caller skips optimistic hide |
| 11 | P2 | reason: "compact" for truncate event | Changed to "truncate" |
| 12 | P2 | Defer local delete until truncate confirmed | Boolean return controls optimistic hide |
| 13 | P2 | Avoid aborting on no-op | Phase 1 confirms cut before Phase 2 abort |
| 14 | P2 | Scope callback to initiating session | Session key captured at dispatch |
| 15 | P2 | Don't clear newer run state | Run-ID guard |
| 16 | P2 | Recompute cutpoint under final lock | Phase 3c re-scans under lock |
| 17 | P2 | Don't gate refresh on unchanged run ID | loadChatHistory called regardless |
| 18 | P2 | Preserve run state on no-op | Only clears when truncated: true |
| 19 | P2 | Restore optimistic hide on no-op/error | onFail() before session guard |
| 20 | P2 | Re-check before aborting | Phase 1 before Phase 2 |
| 21 | P2 | Reload even if newer run starts | loadChatHistory in new-run branch |
| 22 | P2 | Handle ENOENT in phase-1 scan | ENOENT catch with fallback |
| 23 | P2 | Restore delete when off-session | onFail() always called first |
| 24 | P2 | Preserve chat history on RPC fail | .catch only sets lastError |
| 25 | P2 | Comment refers to fromSeq | Updated to p.seq |

## Regression Test Plan

- All 30 method-scope classification tests pass
- All 37 protocol tests pass
- All 38 gateway sessions integration tests pass
- `pnpm build` passes cleanly
- `pnpm format` clean

target.title: cron: reject webhook URLs with embedded credentials
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Cron webhook URLs could include embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.com/hook), which can leak secrets in logs, config, or URLs. - Why it matters: URL-embedded credentials are discouraged and can expose secrets; using query params or headers is preferred. - What changed: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl now rejects URLs with username or password in the parsed URL. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth headers, query params, or other credential handling; only http(s)://user:pass@host URLs are rejected. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Cron webhook URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:secret@host/path) are now rejected as invalid. URLs without credentials continue to work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): CRON webhook delivery - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Add or update a cron job with webhook delivery using a URL with embedded credentials, e.g. https://user:pass@example.invalid/webhook. 2. Observe that the webhook URL is rejected (job will not use it). 3. Use the same job with https://example.invalid/webhook (no credentials) and confirm it is accepted. ### Expected - URLs with user:pass@ in the authority are rejected. - URLs without embedded credentials continue to work. ### Actual - Behavior matches above ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl returns null for URLs like http://user:pass@host/path; returns the URL for http://host/path. - Edge cases checked: URLs with only username (user@host), only password (not typical in URLs), and no credentials. - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end cron job creation via CLI or UI with credential URLs; live webhook delivery. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`No`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Update any cron job webhook URLs that include user:pass@ in the URL. Remove embedded credentials and use headers or query params for auth instead. ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. - Files/config to restore: src/cron/webhook-url.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Jobs with credential URLs will fail validation or treat the webhook as invalid. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Existing jobs using credential URLs will break. - Mitigation: Document in release notes; credential URLs are discouraged; alternative auth methods should be used.

The branch adds `openclaw sessions compact <key>`, rejects CLI-originated `/compact` messages with exit 1, documents the command/RPC, and adds an active-run guard before maxLines transcript truncation.

PR surface: Source +256, Tests +312, Docs +51. Total +619 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows no `openclaw sessions compact` command, the raw `sessions.compact` RPC exists, CLI `/compact` is not rejected before dispatch, and the current maxLines path archives/truncates without the active-run guard.

target.title: feat(cli): add `openclaw sessions compact` and fail loudly on CLI `/compact` (fixes #90640)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90640.

`sessions.compact` was reachable only as an internal Gateway RPC: there was no
CLI command and no docs, and `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` silently
no-opped with exit 0 (the slash-command handler rejects CLI-originated senders,
so the message fell through to an ordinary agent turn that compacted nothing).

This PR closes both gaps:

- Adds `openclaw sessions compact <key>` as a first-class command wrapping the
  existing `sessions.compact` RPC. It exits non-zero whenever the gateway
  reports a failed compaction — both a transport error and an `ok:false`
  payload — so automation never mistakes a silent no-op for success.
- Makes `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` fail loudly: it prints a redirect
  to `openclaw sessions compact <key>` and exits 1 instead of silently exiting
  0. The shared chat-side `/compact` handler is left untouched, so chat
  behaviour and message delivery are unchanged.
- Inherits the parent `sessions` options for `compact`, matching its sibling
  subcommands (`list`/`cleanup`/`info`/…). Previously a parent-level
  `--agent`/`--json` was silently dropped, so `openclaw sessions --agent work
  compact <key>` compacted the **default** agent's session instead of the
  `work` agent's — a wrong-target session-state mutation. The compact-level
  option still takes precedence over the inherited parent option.
- Rejects unsupported inherited parent options for `compact` (`--store`,
  `--all-agents`, `--active`, `--limit`): the gateway resolves the target store
  from `<key>`/`--agent`, so honoring a parent `--store` is impossible and
  silently dropping it could mutate a different store than the user named. These
  are now rejected with a clear error and a non-zero exit instead of ignored.
- Reports an asynchronously *started* Codex compaction (`thread/compact/start`,
  `ok:true` / `compacted:false` / `pending:true`) as pending instead of
  collapsing it into a misleading "No compaction needed" message.
- Guards the `--max-lines` truncate path against active runs. The non-maxLines
  (LLM) compact branch already interrupts an active session run before
  compacting, but the maxLines branch archived and overwrote the transcript in
  place without that guard. Exposing `--max-lines` as a documented CLI flag would
  otherwise make the active-run data-loss mode tracked by #72765 easy to hit, so
  the maxLines branch now runs the same `interruptSessionRunIfActive` guard
  **before** reading the tail, archiving, and truncating — parity with the LLM
  compact path.
- Strictly validates `--max-lines` and `--timeout` (positive integers only).
- Documents the command and the `sessions.compact` RPC in docs/cli/sessions.md.

## Why this is safe

Two scopes change, both narrow:

- **CLI surface** (command registration + `--message` dispatch). This does
  **not** modify the shared `commands-compact.ts` handler that powers chat
  `/compact`, so chat behaviour and message delivery are unchanged — only the
  CLI path that was already broken changes.
- **Gateway `sessions.compact` maxLines branch** gains the active-run interrupt
  guard. This reuses the existing `interruptSessionRunIfActive` helper — the
  exact call the non-maxLines (LLM) compact branch already makes — and runs it in
  the maxLines truncate branch before it reads the tail, archives, and overwrites
  the transcript. It does not redesign compact/session ownership and is scoped to
  active-run parity for the entry point this PR newly exposes; it can supersede
  the narrow guard portion of #72765 for that path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Five behaviors of the latest head are
  exercised. (A) `openclaw sessions compact <key>` drives a real compaction
  end-to-end through the new CLI command body (not just the raw RPC). (B)
  `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` fails loudly with a non-zero exit
  instead of silently exiting 0. (C) a Codex app-server `thread/compact/start`
  result (`ok:true` / `compacted:false` / `pending:true`) is rendered as a
  *started* compaction, not collapsed into "No compaction needed". (D)
  unsupported inherited parent `sessions` options for `compact`
  (`--store`/`--all-agents`/`--active`/`--limit`) are rejected with a clear
  error and a non-zero exit instead of being silently dropped. (E) the maxLines
  truncate path runs the same active-run interrupt guard as the LLM compact
  branch before archiving and truncating, so exposing `--max-lines` from the CLI
  does not make the #72765 data-loss mode easier to hit.

- **Real environment tested**: (A)/(B) drive the **production CLI command
  functions** (`sessionsCompactCommand` and `agentCliCommand`) against a **real
  in-process OpenClaw Gateway server** over a live WebSocket
  (`ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`), with a temporary on-disk session transcript seeded
  with 500 JSONL lines, read back from disk. (C) is split into two parts that
  each cross a real boundary: **(C1) payload source** drives the real Codex
  production function `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` against a real
  on-disk session binding (written with `writeCodexAppServerBinding`, read
  back with `readCodexAppServerBinding`) and an injected app-server client, and
  observes the real `thread/compact/start` pending payload; **(C2) CLI display**
  drives the full real `openclaw sessions compact <key>` path (real Commander
  parse → real action → real `sessionsCompactCommand` → real `describeCompaction`
  → real `console.log`) and feeds it that exact pending payload. C2 is a
  display-only proof: only the `callGatewayCli` transport hop is stubbed (the
  gateway handler passes `result.result.details` through verbatim — see
  `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` `respond(...)`), and C1 already
  proves the pending payload is real. (D) drives a real Commander parse of
  `openclaw sessions --store … compact <key>` and captures the real stderr and
  `process.exit` code.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: (A) Started the real
  Gateway, seeded `agent:main:main` with a 500-line on-disk transcript, then
  invoked the production `sessions compact` command function with
  `key: "agent:main:main"`, `maxLines: 50` — full CLI body → callGateway →
  gateway RPC → filesystem path. (B) Invoked the production `agent` command
  function with `--message '/compact'`. (C1) Wrote a Codex app-server binding to
  disk, called `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession({trigger:"manual", …})`,
  read the binding back from disk. (C2) Ran `openclaw sessions compact
  agent:main:main` through real Commander with the C1 pending payload returned
  by the stubbed transport, and a contrast run returning a genuine no-op. (D)
  Ran `openclaw sessions --store /tmp/other.json compact agent:main:main` and
  `openclaw sessions --all-agents --limit 25 compact agent:work:main` through
  real Commander.

- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal output captured from the real
  command functions on the latest head:

```text
PROOF A — openclaw sessions compact <key>  (real command fn → real gateway over WS)
  url: ws://127.0.0.1:30000
  command stdout (runtime.log):
    Compacted session agent:main:main (kept 50 lines).
    Archived transcript: <tmp>/sess-main.jsonl.bak.2026-06-08T11-08-40.217Z
  command stderr (runtime.error): (none)
  exit code: 0 (process.exit not called = success)
  transcript lines BEFORE compact (on disk): 500
  transcript lines AFTER  compact (on disk): 50
  .bak archive lines (original preserved):   500

PROOF B — openclaw agent --message '/compact'  (real agentCliCommand guard)
  command stderr (runtime.error):
    Slash commands cannot be executed via --message from the CLI.
    Use: openclaw sessions compact <key>
  exit code: 1

PROOF C1 — codex pending payload source  (real maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession, real disk binding)
  on-disk session binding file: <tmp>/session.jsonl
  binding read back from disk (readCodexAppServerBinding):
    {"schemaVersion":1,"threadId":"thread-1","sessionFile":"<tmp>/session.jsonl","cwd":"<tmp>",...}
  codex client request sent: ["thread/compact/start",{"threadId":"thread-1"}]
  returned pending payload:
    {"ok":true,"compacted":false,"result":{"summary":"","firstKeptEntryId":"",
     "tokensBefore":123,"details":{"backend":"codex-app-server","threadId":"thread-1",
     "signal":"thread/compact/start","pending":true}}}

PROOF C2 — codex pending compaction DISPLAY  (real CLI path; only callGatewayCli transport stubbed)
  RPC dispatch reached: callGatewayCli("sessions.compact", {timeout:"180000",json:false}, {key:"agent:main:main"})
  command stdout (real console.log):
    Compaction started for session agent:main:main (pending; completion is reported asynchronously by the backend).
  contrast — genuine no-op (compacted:false, no pending details) stdout:
    No compaction needed for session agent:work:main (already within budget).

PROOF D — reject unsupported inherited parent options  (real Commander parse)
  case 1: openclaw sessions --store /tmp/other.json compact agent:main:main
    stderr: `sessions compact` does not support the parent `sessions` option --store; the gateway resolves the target store from <key> and --agent.
    exit  : 1
  case 2: openclaw sessions --all-agents --limit 25 compact agent:work:main
    stderr: `sessions compact` does not support the parent `sessions` options --all-agents, --limit; the gateway resolves the target store from <key> and --agent.
    exit  : 1

PROOF E — maxLines active-run guard  (real in-process Gateway over WS; committed regression)
  test 1 (no active run): seeded 500-line transcript, maxLines:50
    -> compacted=true, kept=50; on-disk transcript = 50 lines; .bak archive = 500 lines
    -> abortCalls=[], waitCalls=[]            (no run to interrupt)
  test 2 (active embedded run on sess-main): maxLines:50
    -> abortCalls=["sess-main"], waitCalls=["sess-main"]   (same guard as the LLM branch)
    -> then truncated 500 -> 50 on disk
  test 3 (active run cannot be interrupted; wait-for-end returns false): maxLines:50
    -> RPC ok=false BEFORE any archive/truncate; transcript still 500 lines; no .bak created
```

- **Observed result after fix**: (A) The production `sessions compact` command
  printed the success line and `.bak` path, reduced the active transcript from
  500 → 50 lines on disk, and preserved the original 500 lines in the `.bak`
  archive — all through the new CLI command body. (B) `agent --message
  '/compact'` emitted the redirect message on stderr and exited 1. (C) The real
  Codex function produced the `thread/compact/start` pending payload from a real
  on-disk binding (C1), and the real CLI display rendered it as
  `Compaction started for session agent:main:main (pending; …)` while a genuine
  no-op still rendered `No compaction needed …` (C2) — proving the pending
  branch is distinguished from a no-op. (D) Both unsupported-parent-option
  invocations were rejected on stderr with exit 1 before any gateway call. (E)
  With an active embedded run, the maxLines truncate branch fired the same
  abort + wait-for-end interrupt before archiving, then truncated 500 → 50;
  when the run could not be interrupted the RPC failed with the transcript left
  intact at 500 lines and no `.bak` — proving the guard precedes the
  destructive write.

- **What was not tested**: No GUI or screenshot flow. C2 stubs only the
  WS/`callGatewayCli` transport hop (display-only proof); the live transport is
  covered by PROOF A, the gateway handler is verbatim pass-through for
  `result.result.details`, and the pending payload source is covered by C1.

## Tests

- `src/commands/sessions-compact.test.ts` — 6 unit tests: success token delta,
  pending Codex `thread/compact/start` reported as pending (not a no-op),
  `ok:false` → exit 1, RPC throw → exit 1, JSON-mode `ok:false` → payload +
  exit 1, params forwarding (`agentId`/`maxLines`).
- `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` — parametrized cases proving
  `/compact`, `/compact <text>`, `/compact:<text>`, `/COMPACT`, and
  `  /Compact  ` are all rejected with exit 1 before any gateway or embedded
  turn, while `/compacting …` is NOT mistaken for a control command.
- `src/cli/program/register.status-health-sessions.test.ts` — regression
  coverage for parent-option handling: a parent `sessions --agent`/`--json` is
  forwarded to `compact`, a compact-level `--agent` overrides the parent, and an
  unsupported inherited parent `--store`/`--all-agents`/`--active`/`--limit` is
  rejected with a non-zero exit (31 tests pass).
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts` — committed coverage for the
  Codex `thread/compact/start` pending payload source backing PROOF C1: the real
  `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` returns `ok:true` / `compacted:false` with
  `details.backend = "codex-app-server"`, `details.signal =
  "thread/compact/start"`, and `details.pending = true` for a manual trigger.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` — three regression tests for
  the maxLines truncate branch over a real in-process Gateway: (1) with no active
  run, `--max-lines 50` truncates a 500-line on-disk transcript to 50 lines and
  preserves all 500 original lines in the `.bak` archive, aborting nothing; (2)
  with an active embedded run, the maxLines branch runs the same
  `interruptSessionRunIfActive` guard as the LLM compact branch — asserting the
  abort + wait-for-end calls fire — **before** archiving and truncating, then
  still truncates 500 → 50; (3) order proof: when the active run cannot be
  interrupted (wait-for-end returns false), the RPC fails **before** any
  archive/truncate — the transcript is left at 500 lines with no `.bak`,
  confirming the interrupt precedes the destructive write. This is the regression
  coverage requested for the active-run guard finding.

target.title: feat(docker): support offline setup reruns
target.body:
Fixes #70443.

## Summary
- Add `scripts/docker/setup.sh --offline` for airgapped reruns with a preloaded `OPENCLAW_IMAGE`.
- Skip Docker build/pull in offline mode, but still run the normal setup flow: `.env` sync, permission fix, onboarding/config sync, and Compose startup.
- Verify the main image with `docker image inspect`; warn, not fail, when the optional sandbox image is missing.
- Document the offline rerun flow and add Docker setup e2e coverage.

## Test plan
- `bash -n scripts/docker/setup.sh`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/docker-setup.e2e.test.ts` (1 file, 37 tests)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/docker-setup.e2e.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/docker-setup.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check && pnpm docs:list | rg 'install/docker|Docker'`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Offline Docker setup reruns should reuse an already loaded `OPENCLAW_IMAGE`, skip Docker build/pull, and still continue through the normal setup steps and Compose gateway startup.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux Docker Engine 20.10.0 with a temporary Docker Compose v5.1.4 CLI plugin under `DOCKER_CONFIG`, current PR head `9f3ab77974bbfb89e78abb8ce2f8abef1ecac3c3`. The image used for the proof was a preloaded local proof image (`openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local`) that provides the container commands the setup script calls; it was used to prove the offline setup control flow without pulling or building inside `setup.sh --offline`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
proof_dir=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/.proof-89062
run_dir="$proof_dir/run-shell-1780316777"
DOCKER_CONFIG="$proof_dir/docker-cli" docker compose version
DOCKER_CONFIG="$proof_dir/docker-cli" docker image inspect openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local --format 'preloaded image {{.Id}} size={{.Size}}'
start_ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
DOCKER_CONFIG="$proof_dir/docker-cli" \
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR="$run_dir/config" \
OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR="$run_dir/workspace" \
OPENCLAW_AUTH_PROFILE_SECRET_DIR="$run_dir/auth" \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=redacted-proof-token \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19672 \
OPENCLAW_BRIDGE_PORT=19673 \
OPENCLAW_MSTEAMS_PORT=19674 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_ONBOARDING=1 \
bash scripts/docker/setup.sh --offline >"$run_dir/stdout.log" 2>"$run_dir/stderr.log"
status=$?
end_ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
echo "STATUS=$status"
echo "START=$start_ts END=$end_ts"
sed -n '1,120p' "$run_dir/stdout.log"
sed -n '1,120p' "$run_dir/stderr.log"
DOCKER_CONFIG="$proof_dir/docker-cli" \
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR="$run_dir/config" \
OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR="$run_dir/workspace" \
OPENCLAW_AUTH_PROFILE_SECRET_DIR="$run_dir/auth" \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=redacted-proof-token \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19672 \
OPENCLAW_BRIDGE_PORT=19673 \
OPENCLAW_MSTEAMS_PORT=19674 \
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml ps
docker events --since "$start_ts" --until "$end_ts" --filter image=openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local --format '{{.Time}} {{.Type}} {{.Action}} {{.Actor.Attributes.name}} {{.Actor.Attributes.image}}' | rg -i 'pull|build|tag|import|load' || true
docker events --since "$start_ts" --until "$end_ts" --filter image=openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local --format '{{.Time}} {{.Type}} {{.Action}} {{.Actor.Attributes.name}} {{.Actor.Attributes.image}}' | sed -n '1,80p'
```
- Evidence after fix:
```text
Docker Compose version v5.1.4
preloaded image sha256:95f6c1838cd99781ada3b570f4b2619ed36fe2351f4e5ab0c35f94de9d34ca52 size=74842430
STATUS=0
START=2026-06-01T12:26:17Z END=2026-06-01T12:26:25Z
--- stdout
==> Using preloaded Docker image: openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local

==> Fixing data-directory permissions

==> Skipping onboarding (OPENCLAW_SKIP_ONBOARDING is set)

Set gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins to ["http://localhost:19672","http://127.0.0.1:19672"] for non-loopback bind.

==> Provider setup (optional)
WhatsApp (QR):
  docker compose -f .../docker-compose.yml run --rm openclaw-cli channels login
Telegram (bot token):
  docker compose -f .../docker-compose.yml run --rm openclaw-cli channels add --channel telegram --token <token>
Discord (bot token):
  docker compose -f .../docker-compose.yml run --rm openclaw-cli channels add --channel discord --token <token>
Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels

==> Starting gateway

Gateway running with host port mapping.
Access from tailnet devices via the host's tailnet IP.
Config: <proof-run>/config
Workspace: <proof-run>/workspace
Token: stored in Docker environment/config (not printed).

Commands:
  docker compose -f .../docker-compose.yml logs -f openclaw-gateway
  docker compose -f .../docker-compose.yml exec openclaw-gateway sh -lc 'node dist/index.js health --token "$OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN"'
--- stderr
Network openclaw-issue-70443_default Creating
Network openclaw-issue-70443_default Created
Container openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-c4ed4ff582fb Creating
Container openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-c4ed4ff582fb Created
Container openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-a9f88f0931d1 Creating
Container openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-a9f88f0931d1 Created

--- compose ps
NAME                                      IMAGE                                COMMAND                  SERVICE            CREATED         STATUS                           PORTS
openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-1   openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local   "node dist/index.js ..."  openclaw-gateway   3 seconds ago   Up 1 second (health: starting)   0.0.0.0:19674->3978/tcp, 0.0.0.0:19672->18789/tcp, 0.0.0.0:19673->18790/tcp
--- docker events: pull/build/load during setup window
(no output)
--- docker events: container lifecycle during setup window
1780316778 container create openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-c4ed4ff582fb openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local
1780316779 container start openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-c4ed4ff582fb openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local

1780316783 container start openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-a9f88f0931d1 openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local
1780316783 container die openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-run-a9f88f0931d1 openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local
1780316783 container create openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-1 openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local
1780316784 container start openclaw-issue-70443-openclaw-gateway-1 openclaw-offline-proof-shell:local
```
- Observed result after fix: `scripts/docker/setup.sh --offline` reused the preloaded local image, printed the offline preloaded-image path, ran permission repair and gateway config sync, reached `==> Starting gateway`, and real Docker Compose started `openclaw-gateway`. The Docker event query for the setup window produced no pull/build/load events for the image, while container lifecycle events show the Compose run/start path.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full production OpenClaw image because `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` did not have a manifest in this environment and building the full OpenClaw Docker image is substantially heavier. Interactive onboarding was skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_ONBOARDING=1`; the existing setup e2e tests cover the onboarding branch with stubs.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a real Docker/Compose proof with a preloaded local proof image, not a full application image. It validates the changed offline setup control flow (local image inspect, no build/pull, downstream setup steps, and Compose startup) but not the full OpenClaw runtime inside the container.

## Not run
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --label docker-setup-offline-check --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Local `crabbox` was not usable in this checkout, so I could not run the wrapped changed-check.

target.title: fix(feishu): parse interactive card post-format fallback content
target.body:
## Summary

Fix `parseInteractiveCardContent()` to handle Feishu API post-format fallback content for interactive card messages.

Fixes #60380
Related: #41609 #48281

## Problem

When reading card messages via `GET /im/v1/messages/{message_id}`, Feishu returns `body.content` in a **post-style fallback format** — nested arrays with `tag:"text"` elements — instead of the original card template JSON. The existing parser only handled card template format (`div`/`markdown` tags), causing all fallback content to silently return `"[Interactive Card]"`.

**Before:** Any `message(action=read)` on a card → `"[Interactive Card]"`
**After:** Extracts title + text content from both card template and post-format fallback

## Example

API returns this for a card message:
```json
{
  "title": "🤖 clawdbot",
  "elements": [[
    {"tag": "img", "image_key": "img_v3_02ad_..."},
    {"tag": "text", "text": "Daily report summary"},
    {"tag": "text", "text": "13 people missing reports"}
  ]]
}
```

- **Before:** `"[Interactive Card]"`
- **After:** `"🤖 clawdbot\nDaily report summary\n13 people missing reports"`

## Changes

**`extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`**
- Handle nested arrays in `elements` (post-format fallback `[[...]]`)
- Extract `tag:"text"` and `tag:"a"` elements from fallback content
- Extract card title from `header.title.content` (template) or top-level `title` (fallback)
- Support `tag:"plain_text"` elements
- Return title even when elements array is missing

**`extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`**
- Add test: post-format fallback with nested arrays and title
- Add test: card template with `header.title.content`
- Add test: link extraction from `tag:"a"` elements with href

## Testing

- [x] Verified with real Feishu API responses from production bot
- [x] Existing card template format tests still pass (div/markdown)
- [x] 3 new test cases covering fallback scenarios

target.title: fix(sessions): cache warm transcript reads to avoid per-turn re-parse
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83943.

The session resource loader re-parsed the entire transcript on every warm turn. `loadEntriesFromFile` ran `readFileSync` + line-by-line `JSON.parse` over the whole JSONL each time `SessionManager.open` was called, so the per-warm-turn cost grew O(n) with transcript length. The reporter measured the session-resource-loader stage climbing from ~14.5s → 31.5s → 42.3s across consecutive warm turns of a single session, eventually tripping a WebSocket timeout.

## What changed

**`src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts`** — add a bounded (max 8) snapshot-keyed cache for `loadEntriesFromFile`. The cache key is a high-resolution file snapshot (`dev`/`ino`/`size` + nanosecond `mtime`/`ctime` via `statSync({ bigint: true })`); a warm open whose snapshot still matches returns the cached entries without re-reading or re-parsing the file. Only current-version (v3) transcripts are cached — older versions still go through migration with fresh mutable entries, so cache hits never share objects that migrate. Cached entries are deep-frozen and handed out as a shallow array copy (`.slice()`), so callers cannot mutate the cached prefix and manager appends land on a separate array, while avoiding a per-open deep clone. Appends update the cache incrementally; the cache invalidates when the file snapshot changes (external replace / another writer).

**`src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts`** — 7 tests: warm reuse without re-parse, append without stale readback, cache invalidation on external file replacement, invalidation when another writer appends before this manager persists, and old-version transcripts still migrating while bypassing the cache.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `loadEntriesFromFile` no longer re-parses the whole transcript on every warm `SessionManager.open`. Warm opens of an unchanged current-version session reuse cached entries, eliminating the per-warm-turn O(n) re-parse cost, while session correctness (no stale or shared-mutation, cache invalidation on external change) is preserved.

**Real environment tested:** Local worktree of `origin/main` at base `ca9249e357291890125a5a801a5681defbc816ff` with this patch applied, Node v22.22.0. Drove the real exported `loadEntriesFromFile` via `node --import tsx` — the actual production loader, no mocks. `JSON.parse` call count measured by wrapping the global parse around the loop.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# Real production loader, 4000-entry transcript, 12 warm opens:
node --import tsx ./proof-83943.mts
# Baseline measured by git-stashing this patch and re-running the same harness.

# Regression suite:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Real output driving the production `loadEntriesFromFile` over a 4000-entry transcript, 12 warm opens:
```
# AFTER (this patch):
transcript entries = 4000 (+1 session header), warm opens = 12
entries returned per open = 4001 (correctness: stable across opens)
JSON.parse calls = 4001
wall time = 45.0ms

# BASELINE (origin/main, this patch stashed):
JSON.parse calls = 48012
wall time = 198.0ms
```

**Observed result after fix:** `JSON.parse` calls drop from 48012 (12 × full re-parse) to 4001 (one initial parse; the 11 warm hits do zero parsing) — a 91.7% reduction — and wall time drops from 198.0ms to 45.0ms (~4.4× faster). Every open still returns all 4001 entries, so correctness is preserved.

**What was not tested:** No live end-to-end Windows + Feishu + MiniMax reproduction of the original ~42s stall (that environment is unavailable); the change is in the deterministic transcript loader, exercised directly with the real exported function and disk readback. This PR addresses only the per-warm-turn transcript-growth subpath, not the constant `reload()` baseline cost discussed in #82536.

## Validation (supplemental)

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts` → `Tests 7 passed (7)`
- full `src/agents/sessions/` suite → session-manager and related tests green
- `tsgo:core:test` → no new errors in changed files; `oxlint` → clean

target.title: fix(plugins): serialize binding approval saves
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Serializes plugin-binding approval persistence so overlapping `allow-always` saves cannot write stale approval state after a newer save updates the in-memory cache.
- Keeps the existing in-memory cache behavior immediate for callers, while queueing disk writes behind the previous approval save.
- Adds a regression test that holds the first write open, starts a second approval save, and verifies the second write waits and the final file contains both scopes.

Why does this matter now?

#64065 remains open because Race C can lose persistent plugin-binding approvals on restart when concurrent approval saves race on `~/.openclaw/plugin-binding-approvals.json`.

What is the intended outcome?

Concurrent persistent plugin-binding approvals finish with disk state matching the latest in-memory approval file.

What is intentionally out of scope?

Race A and Race B from #64065 are left unchanged because the issue review already classifies them as mitigated by existing behavior.

What does success look like?

Two overlapping `allow-always` approvals can both resolve successfully and the persisted approval file still contains the expected approvals after both writes complete.

What should reviewers focus on?

The `saveApprovals` queueing behavior and the out-of-order write regression coverage in `conversation-binding.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #64065

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #81402

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

The ClawSweeper review on #64065 requested a focused Race C repair with regression coverage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: overlapping plugin-binding `allow-always` approval saves should not leave `plugin-binding-approvals.json` missing a newer approval scope.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with real `requestPluginConversationBinding` / `resolvePluginConversationBindingApproval` modules, real session-binding adapters, and a real temporary HOME approval file.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module` script that registered Discord adapters for `default` and `work`, requested two plugin bindings, resolved both with `allow-always` via `Promise.all`, then read `.openclaw/plugin-binding-approvals.json` from the temporary HOME.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
[plugins/binding] plugin binding requested plugin=codex root=/plugins/codex-a channel=discord account=default conversation=proof-channel-1
[plugins/binding] plugin binding requested plugin=codex root=/plugins/codex-a channel=discord account=work conversation=proof-channel-2
[plugins/binding] plugin binding approved plugin=codex root=/plugins/codex-a decision=allow-always channel=discord account=default conversation=proof-channel-1
[plugins/binding] plugin binding approved plugin=codex root=/plugins/codex-a decision=allow-always channel=discord account=work conversation=proof-channel-2
{
  "results": [
    "approved",
    "approved"
  ],
  "approvalsPath": "/media/vdc/0668001470/tmp/openclaw-approval-proof-kiz6XW/.openclaw/plugin-binding-approvals.json",
  "approvals": [
    {
      "pluginRoot": "/plugins/codex-a",
      "channel": "discord",
      "accountId": "default"
    },
    {
      "pluginRoot": "/plugins/codex-a",
      "channel": "discord",
      "accountId": "work"
    }
  ],
  "approvalCount": 2
}
```

- Observed result after fix: both approval resolutions returned `approved`, and the real approval file contained both `default` and `work` approval scopes after the overlapping save path completed.
- What was not tested: the broad database-first runtime-state PR path in #81402 and the already-mitigated Race A/Race B surfaces.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local `crabbox` failed its basic binary sanity check, so the remote-wrapper form of `check:changed` could not start from this machine; I ran the local changed-check child path directly as supplemental validation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #64065 documents the pre-fix interleaving where a first stale `writeJson` can complete after a newer in-memory approval state.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/conversation-binding.ts src/plugins/conversation-binding.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/plugins/conversation-binding.ts src/plugins/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `serializes overlapping always-allow approval writes`, which blocks the first approval write, starts a second approval, verifies the second approval is still waiting, then confirms the persisted approval file contains both approval scopes after the queue drains.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before this fix, competing `saveApprovals` calls could await independent `writeJson` calls, allowing an older file snapshot to persist after newer in-memory approval state was already visible.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Persistent plugin approval saving now waits behind an earlier approval save, so a slow or failed earlier disk write can delay later approval persistence.

How is that risk mitigated?

Each caller still receives its own write error if its save fails, the chain catches prior failures before accepting later writes, and the cache remains updated synchronously before disk persistence just like before.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Nothing known from the author side.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the ClawSweeper #64065 recommendation to land a narrow Race C repair with regression coverage.

target.title: fix(doctor): separate platform-incompatible skills from missing requirements
target.body:
Fixes #89232

## Summary

`openclaw doctor` reported skills with a declared OS requirement that doesn't match the current platform (e.g. macOS-only `apple-notes`, `imsg`, `homebrew` on Linux/Windows) under the same "Missing requirements" count as genuinely-broken installs, and `doctor --fix` would prompt to disable them. Those skills aren't broken — they're inapplicable by design on this OS — and the noise hides real issues.

This derives a `platformIncompatible` flag from the **shared requirement evaluator's `missing.os`** (which already accounts for remote macOS node eligibility, not just local `process.platform`) and updates doctor so platform-mismatched skills are:
- excluded from the "Missing requirements" count and surfaced as a single `Incompatible (platform mismatch, auto-skipped): N` line;
- excluded from `doctor --fix`'s disable target, so it no longer prompts to disable inapplicable-but-not-broken skills.

- `src/skills/discovery/status.ts` — expose `platformIncompatible` from the shared evaluator's `missing.os` (incl. remote macOS eligibility)
- `src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.ts` — drop platform-incompatible from "Missing requirements", add single rollup line
- `src/commands/doctor-skills-core.ts` — exclude platform-incompatible from `collectUnavailableAgentSkills` (the `doctor --fix` disable target)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** A skill whose declared OS requirement excludes this host (and that no remote node can satisfy) is no longer counted as a "missing requirement" nor offered for disable by `doctor --fix`; it is reported once under an "Incompatible (platform mismatch)" rollup, so genuinely-broken installs stay visible. Skills satisfiable via a remote macOS node are not flagged, because the flag follows the shared evaluator's `missing.os`.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22, HEAD `455ff07c7bde7b4512055682ba468a03048f2535` based on `origin/main` `489efc8f5eb15066f007f820ea71398357ad08e8`. Production functions `buildWorkspaceSkillStatus` and `collectUnavailableAgentSkills` driven directly via `node --import tsx`, plus the Vitest suites.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
node --import tsx -e 'Promise.all([import("./src/skills/discovery/status.ts"), import("./src/skills/test-support/test-helpers.ts"), import("./src/commands/doctor-skills-core.ts")]).then(([m,h,d])=>{
  const mk=(n,meta)=>({skill:h.createCanonicalFixtureSkill({name:n,description:"t",filePath:"/tmp/"+n,baseDir:"/tmp",source:"test"}),frontmatter:{},metadata:meta});
  const r=m.buildWorkspaceSkillStatus("/tmp/ws",{entries:[mk("mac-only",{os:["darwin"],requires:{}}),mk("portable",{})]});
  for(const s of r.skills) console.log(s.skillKey,"platformIncompatible="+s.platformIncompatible);
  console.log("collectUnavailable:",JSON.stringify(d.collectUnavailableAgentSkills(r).map(s=>s.skillKey)));
  console.log("Missing:",r.skills.filter(s=>!s.eligible&&!s.disabled&&!s.blockedByAllowlist&&!s.platformIncompatible).length);
})'

OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/skills/discovery/status.test.ts \
  src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts \
  src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts --reporter=dot
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
platform: linux
  mac-only  platformIncompatible=true   (via missing.os)
  portable  platformIncompatible=false  (via missing.os)
collectUnavailable (doctor --fix disable target): []   <- mac-only excluded
Missing requirements: 0

status.test.ts: 12 passed | doctor-workspace-status.test.ts: 6 passed | doctor-skills.test.ts: 9 passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** The macOS-only skill on Linux resolves to `platformIncompatible=true` (via the shared evaluator's `missing.os`), drops out of both the doctor "Missing requirements" count (1 → 0) and the `doctor --fix` disable target (`collectUnavailableAgentSkills` → `[]`), and appears once under the "Incompatible (platform mismatch)" rollup. A portable skill stays `false`. `tsgo:core` passes.

**What was not tested:** A live full `openclaw doctor` / `doctor --fix` run on Windows with the real ClawHub bundle (the reporter's environment), and an actual reachable remote macOS node; the platform-mismatch + remote-eligibility branch is exercised on Linux against macOS-only skills via the production status builder, the doctor disable collector, and the unit suites — the same code path.
